basic_details.job_title: AIIMS Delhi Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 26 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi (AIIMS Delhi)
basic_details.short_information: All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi (AIIMS Delhi) has released the official Assistant Professor Notification 2026 on April 2026 at aiims.edu. A total of 26 vacancies have been announced under the AIIMS Delhi Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between up to 50 years with a qualification of Medical qualification (MD/MS/DM/M.Ch.) and teaching/research experience can apply online from 27-04-2026 to 11-05-2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

application_fee.sc_fee: Rs. 2400 (Refundable for those who appear in the interview)

age_limit.age_details: Max: 50 years (General), with relaxations for SC/ST (5 years), OBC (3 years), PWBD (5 years), Government Servant (5 years), Ex-Servicemen (5 years). Max age after relaxation is 55 years.
age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 50 years for General category candidates. Age relaxation is applicable as per government rules: SC/ST: 05 years OBC: 03 years PWBD: 05 years Government Servant: 05 years Ex-Servicemen: 05 years Maximum age after relaxation is 55 years.

qualification.education_qualification: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, with specific postgraduate qualifications (MD/MS/DM/M.Ch.) and teaching/research experience as per the post.
qualification.details: Assistant Professor of Nephrology: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. D.M. in Nephrology (2, 3, 5, or 6 years recognized course) or equivalent. One year teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the Degree of D.M. in Nephrology (2 or 5 years recognized course) or equivalent. No experience is necessary for candidates possessing three or six years recognized Degree of D.M. in Nephrology. Assistant Professor of Community Medicine: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. MD in Community Medicine/ Preventive & Social Medicine or equivalent. Three years teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Community Medicine/Preventive & Social Medicine or equivalent. Assistant Professor of Cardiology: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. D.M. in Cardiology (2, 3, 5, or 6 years recognized course) or equivalent. One year teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the Degree of D.M. in Cardiology (2 or 5 years recognized course) or equivalent. No experience is necessary for candidates possessing three or six years recognized Degree of D.M. in Cardiology. Assistant Professor of Pathology/Cyto-Pathology: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. M.D. in Pathology or equivalent. Three years teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Pathology or equivalent. Assistant Professor of Ophthalmology (Dr. RPC): Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. M.D./M.S. in Ophthalmology or equivalent. Three years teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of M.D./M.S. in Ophthalmology or equivalent. Assistant Professor of Clinical Hematology: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. M.D. in Medicine/ Paediatrics or equivalent followed by D.M. in Cl. Hematology (02 or 03 years recognized course) or equivalent. One year teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the Degree of D.M. in Cl. Hematology (02 years recognized course) following M.D. in Medicine/ Paediatrics or equivalent. No experience is necessary for candidates possessing three years recognized Degree of D.M. in Cl. Hematology. Assistant Professor of Pulmonary Medicine: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. D.M. in Pulmonary Medicine (2, 3, 5, or 6 years recognized course) or equivalent. One year teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the Degree of D.M. in Pulmonary Medicine (2 or 5 years recognized course) or equivalent. No experience is necessary for candidates possessing 03 or 06 years recognized Degree of D.M. in Pulmonary Medicine. Assistant Professor of Onco-Anesthesiology/Anesthesiology: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. M.D in Anesthesiology or equivalent. Three years teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the qualifying degree of MD in Anesthesiology or equivalent. Assistant Professor of Urology: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. M.Ch. in Urology (2, 3, 5, or 6 years recognized course) or equivalent. One year teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the Degree of M.Ch. in Urology (2 or 5 years recognized course) or equivalent. No experience is necessary for candidates possessing 03 or 06 years recognized Degree of M.Ch. in Urology. Assistant Professor of Neurology: Medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956. D.M. in Neurology (2, 3, 5, or 6 years recognized course) or equivalent. One year teaching and/or research experience in a recognized Institution in the subject of specialty after obtaining the Degree of D.M. in Neurology (2 or 5 years recognized course) or equivalent. No experience is necessary for candidates possessing three or six years recognized Degree of D.M. in Neurology.

salary_details.details: Consolidated remuneration of Rs. 1,42,506/- per month.

selection_process: Initial Eligibility Check Screening Process based on objective criteria Interview
how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the AIIMS Delhi Various Posts Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 11-05-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of AIIMS Delhi to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the AIIMS Delhi Assistant Professor posts?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online for the AIIMS Delhi Assistant Professor recruitment is 11 May 2026. Ensure your application is submitted before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the salary offered for the Assistant Professor position?
faq.answer: The selected candidates for the Assistant Professor post will receive a consolidated remuneration of Rs. 1,42,506/- per month.

faq.question: Q3. What are the minimum educational qualifications required?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a medical qualification included in Schedule I& II or part II of the third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act of 1956, along with specific postgraduate qualifications (MD/MS/DM/M.Ch.) and relevant teaching/research experience.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation is provided for SC/ST (5 years), OBC (3 years), PWBD (5 years), Government Servants (5 years), and Ex-Servicemen (5 years). The maximum age after relaxation is 55 years.

faq.question: Q5. How many vacancies are available for Assistant Professor posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 26 vacancies for Assistant Professor posts across various departments at AIIMS Delhi.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for different categories?
faq.answer: The application fee varies: Rs. 3000 for General/OBC, Rs. 2400 for EWS/SC/ST candidates (refundable for SC/ST who appear for interview), and it is exempted for PWBD candidates.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for these posts?
faq.answer: The selection process involves an Initial Eligibility Check, followed by a Screening Process based on objective criteria, and finally an Interview.

faq.question: Q8. Can candidates apply through offline mode?
faq.answer: No, applications must be submitted strictly through the ON-LINE mode only. Offline applications will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q9. What is the job type for these Assistant Professor positions?
faq.answer: These Assistant Professor positions are on a Contract basis.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The direct download link for the official notification PDF is available in the "Important Links" section of this article.
